Good morning, Eliane. Thanks for joining. I will facilitate today, and we will keep minutes so nothing gets lost.
Thank you, Mark. Could you tell me how long the meeting will run?
About forty minutes. Please feel free to weigh in at any point.
I appreciate that. I was wondering if you could walk me through the agenda first.
Of course. Three items: the timeline, the budget, and the training plan. This is a cross-functional project, so we need everyone aligned.
That makes sense. Do you know whether the finance team reached a consensus on the budget?
Not yet. Let us table that point and circle back once they touch base with us.
Good idea. Would you mind explaining who will run the training breakout session?
Your team will lead it. I think we are on the same page. Let us adjourn, and I will send the minutes this afternoon.
Good morning, everyone. I will facilitate today, and I will keep the minutes.
I was wondering if you could weigh in on the budget.
Let us table that point and circle back next week.
Are we all on the same page? If so, let us adjourn.

Hi Eliane, this is Robert. I lead project management for a medical-technology company here in Boston, and I run a lot of cross-functional meetings. Let me share how I keep them productive. First, I always send an agenda the day before, so nobody is surprised. During the meeting, I facilitate: I keep us on time, and I make sure the quiet people weigh in, because the best idea often comes from them. If a topic is not ready, I table it and circle back the following week, rather than force a bad decision. At the end, before we adjourn, I check that we have a real consensus and that everyone is on the same page. Then I send the minutes within an hour.

Hi Eliane, it is Karen from the Austin team, following up after this morning's meeting. I just wanted to touch base. Could you let me know whether your clinical team reached a consensus on the training plan? I was also wondering if you could tell me who will facilitate the breakout session next week. There is no rush, but the sooner we are aligned, the sooner we can circle back to the finance team with a clear timeline. I have attached the minutes from today, so we are all on the same page. Feel free to weigh in by email if anything is unclear. Thanks so much, and talk soon.
